message:
chore(beads): revise remaining bd-nqqq beads with safer approach and comprehensive testing

Key revisions:
- bd-nqqq.6: DO NOT replace show_quit_confirm (Esc behavior incompatible
  with Confirm modal). Instead wire Confirm into PagesWizard export or
  just remove dead_code annotations with documentation.
- bd-nqqq.5: Added 7 specific test cases including modal guards and
  search-mode P-key isolation.
- bd-nqqq.7: Added item-by-item methodology and acceptance criteria for
  each dead_code annotation.
- bd-nqqq.8: Expanded to 5-phase verification with 24 checklist items
  including functional verification of --check-update behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-mdc5","title":"Improve analysis/causal.rs unit test coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T03:15:30.898019932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:17:20.869066652Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T03:17:20.867365948Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-nqqq","title":"Epic: Resolve all stubs, placeholders, and dead code identified in codebase audit","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:28:11.321458487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:28:11.321458487Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.1","title":"Remove asupersync-runtime feature flag and phantom dependency","description":"BACKGROUND: Cargo.toml declares 'asupersync-runtime' feature (line 40) with dep:asupersync (line 19), but ZERO Rust source files reference #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")] anywhere. This was documented as 'post-parity optional async orchestration' but no backing code exists.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Remove from Cargo.toml: the asupersync dependency line and the feature flag lines
2. Keep [features] section with just default = [] or remove if empty
3. Update AGENTS.md Feature Flags section (line 93-96) to remove or note no feature flags exist
4. Verify: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets

JUSTIFICATION: Per AGENTS.md 'no tech debt' policy. Feature can be re-declared when actual async code is written. Phantom dependency inflates compile times.

FILES: Cargo.toml, AGENTS.md
RISK: Very low — nothing references this feature.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:28:34.540971307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:46.516329384Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:28:34.540971307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.2","title":"Remove --update, --rollback, and --yes CLI flags","description":"BACKGROUND: The Go bv had self-update (--update), rollback (--rollback), and confirmation (--yes) flags. The Rust port cannot self-update (cargo install model), so these three flags just print 'not supported' and exit with code 2. They are explicit feature stubs that mislead users.

WHAT TO DO:
1. src/cli.rs: Remove 'update', 'rollback', 'yes' fields from CliArgs struct. Simplify is_operational_command() to just check self.check_update. Remove/update parse_operational_flags tests.
2. src/main.rs: In handle_operational_commands() (lines 2691-2773), remove the cli.update branch, cli.rollback branch, cli.yes check, and the action_count>1 conflict check. Simplify to just handling check_update.
3. tests/admin_cli.rs: Remove tests update_exits_with_remediation, yes_without_update_is_rejected, conflicting_operational_actions_are_rejected. Keep check_update test (updated in separate bead).
4. Verify: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test admin_cli && cargo clippy --all-targets

JUSTIFICATION: Per AGENTS.md — no backwards compatibility concerns, no users. These flags error out anyway. Removing them is cleaner than keeping dead stubs. Users needing updates use 'cargo install --git ...' directly.

FILES: src/cli.rs, src/main.rs, tests/admin_cli.rs
RISK: Low — flags currently just print errors. No functionality lost.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:28:47.053385970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:28:47.053385970Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.2","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:28:47.053385970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.3","title":"Implement actual version check for --check-update","description":"BACKGROUND: --check-update currently prints 'Automatic self-update checks are not implemented' — a stub. Unlike --update/--rollback (which are fundamentally incompatible with cargo install), version CHECKING is achievable and valuable.

DEPENDS ON: bd-nqqq.2 (simplifies handle_operational_commands first)

APPROACH: Shell out to curl to query crates.io or GitHub releases API. Zero new Rust dependencies needed since serde_json is already available for parsing.

WHAT TO DO:
1. src/main.rs: Replace the check_update stub in handle_operational_commands() with:
   a. Run: std::process::Command::new("curl").args(["-sS", "--max-time", "5", "https://api.github.com/repos/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer_rust/releases/latest"])
   b. Parse JSON response, extract tag_name (strip leading 'v' if present)
   c. Compare with env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") using semver-style comparison
   d. Print "Up to date (v{current})" or "Newer version available: v{latest} (you have v{current})\n  Update: cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer_rust.git bvr"
   e. If curl unavailable or network error: print "Could not check for updates (network unavailable).\n  Current version: bvr v{current}"
   f. Always exit 0 (checking should not fail the process)
2. Extract version comparison into a small helper fn compare_versions(current: &str, latest: &str) -> Ordering for testability
3. Add unit tests for compare_versions: equal, newer available, current is newer (dev build), malformed versions
4. tests/admin_cli.rs: Update check_update test to accept either "Up to date" OR "Could not check" (CI may lack network)

WHY GITHUB API OVER CRATES.IO: bvr may not be published on crates.io yet. GitHub releases always exist. The API is public, no auth needed, and rate-limited at 60/hr which is ample for a CLI check.

FILES: src/main.rs, tests/admin_cli.rs
RISK: Medium — network dependency in a CLI tool. Mitigated by 5s timeout and graceful fallback.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:04.890758819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:21.605173774Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.3","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:04.890758819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.3","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:21.604439454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.4","title":"Wire Tutorial modal to Ctrl+T keybinding in TUI","description":"BACKGROUND: The Tutorial modal overlay (ModalOverlay::Tutorial) is FULLY IMPLEMENTED — it renders a welcome screen with keybinding hints (src/tui.rs line 2592-2607) and handles any-key-to-dismiss (line 4221-4224). But open_tutorial() at line 5546 is #[allow(dead_code)] because no production keybinding invokes it. Users can never see this tutorial.

WHAT TO DO:
1. src/tui.rs: Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from open_tutorial() (line 5546)
2. Add keybinding in the global key handler (around line 4558 area — the section that handles keys regardless of view mode):
   KeyCode::Char('t') if modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) => { self.open_tutorial(); }
   IMPORTANT: Verify Ctrl+T is not already bound by checking all KeyCode::Char('t') matches with CTRL modifier
3. Add ("Ctrl+T", "Tutorial") to the help text Global section (around line 7970)
4. Add one integration test: simulate Ctrl+T press from main view, assert modal_overlay == Some(ModalOverlay::Tutorial), then simulate any key press, assert modal_overlay == None

NOTES: The Tutorial content is static text defined at line 2593-2598. It covers modes (b/i/g/h), filters (o/c/r/a), navigation (j/k/Tab), and other keys (?/s/Enter/Esc/q). This is useful for first-time users but doesn't need to be auto-shown on first run (that can be a future enhancement).

FILES: src/tui.rs
RISK: Low — modal system is battle-tested (PagesWizard, RecipePicker already work). Just adding one more trigger.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:20.414566810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:20.414566810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.4","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:20.414566810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.5","title":"Wire PagesWizard modal to P keybinding in TUI","description":"BACKGROUND: The PagesWizard is a COMPLETE multi-step interactive wizard for configuring static pages export. It has full rendering (line 2623-2644), key handling (line 4240 + handle_pages_wizard_key at line 7779), step navigation, and option toggles. open_pages_wizard() at line 5560 and PagesWizardState::new() at line 2280 are both #[allow(dead_code)] because no keybinding invokes them. The wizard is only reachable from tests.

WHAT TO DO:
1. src/tui.rs: Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from open_pages_wizard() (line 5560) and PagesWizardState::new() (line 2281)
2. Add keybinding: KeyCode::Char('P') => { self.open_pages_wizard(); }
   Location: In the global key handler section. Uppercase P avoids conflict with lowercase p (priority hints toggle in Main mode).
   IMPORTANT: Verify P is not already bound.
3. Add ("P", "Pages export wizard") to the help text Actions section (around line 7933)
4. Add one integration test: simulate P press, assert modal_overlay matches Some(ModalOverlay::PagesWizard(_))

NOTES: The wizard has 4 steps (step_count=4) controlled by PagesWizardState fields: step, export_dir, title, include_closed, include_history. Tab cycles steps, Enter confirms, Esc cancels. All tested thoroughly already (lines 21836-21927).

FILES: src/tui.rs
RISK: Low — same modal overlay system used by RecipePicker.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:32.020185201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:32.020185201Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.5","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:32.020185201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.6","title":"Replace show_quit_confirm boolean with Confirm modal overlay","description":"BACKGROUND: ModalOverlay::Confirm is FULLY IMPLEMENTED with rendering (line 2609-2621: shows title, message, Y/N prompt with SemanticTone::Danger styling) and key handling (line 4226-4238: Y confirms, N/Esc cancels, stores result in modal_confirm_result). But open_confirm() at line 5551 is #[allow(dead_code)] — nothing invokes it.

Meanwhile, quit confirmation uses a SEPARATE, simpler mechanism: a show_quit_confirm boolean field on BvrApp (line ~4177-4186) with inline key handling. This is redundant when a proper Confirm modal exists.

WHAT TO DO:
1. src/tui.rs: Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from open_confirm() and from the Confirm variant (lines 5551, 2247)
2. Replace the quit-confirm flow:
   a. When q is pressed (currently sets show_quit_confirm = true): instead call self.open_confirm("Quit bvr?", "Are you sure you want to quit?")
   b. After Confirm modal resolves (modal_confirm_result == Some(true)): return Cmd::Quit
   c. After Confirm modal resolves (modal_confirm_result == Some(false) or None): continue normally
3. Remove the show_quit_confirm field from BvrApp struct
4. Remove show_quit_confirm: false from ALL construction sites (search for 'show_quit_confirm' — expect 15+ matches in test helpers and the main constructor)
5. Remove the show_quit_confirm rendering block and key handling block
6. Update all tests that reference show_quit_confirm to use the Confirm modal pattern instead

THIS IS THE HIGHEST-RISK TASK because it touches the BvrApp struct and all construction sites. Approach methodically:
- First: grep for all 'show_quit_confirm' occurrences and list them
- Second: remove the field and fix all compilation errors
- Third: run full test suite

JUSTIFICATION: Eliminates redundant quit-confirm mechanism in favor of the general-purpose Confirm modal that was built for exactly this purpose. Net reduction in code and BvrApp field count.

FILES: src/tui.rs (extensive changes across struct, construction sites, key handlers, render, tests)
RISK: Medium-high due to 15+ construction site changes. Mitigated by compiler catching all missing fields.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:49.091392631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:49.091392631Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:29:49.091392631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.7","title":"Clean up remaining #[allow(dead_code)] annotations on active code","description":"BACKGROUND: After wiring up Tutorial (bd-nqqq.4), PagesWizard (bd-nqqq.5), and Confirm (bd-nqqq.6), several #[allow(dead_code)] annotations should be removable. This task is the cleanup sweep.

DEPENDS ON: bd-nqqq.4, bd-nqqq.5, bd-nqqq.6 (those tasks remove specific annotations; this catches anything missed)

WHAT TO DO:
1. Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from FlatFileEntry struct (line 2115) — all fields ARE read (at lines 13427, 13431, 5596). Test: remove annotation, run clippy. If no warning fires, the annotation was unnecessary.
2. Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from ModalOverlay::Tutorial variant (line 2244) — should now be used after bd-nqqq.4
3. Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from tokens module (line 696) — 133+ call sites exist. Remove annotation, check if clippy warns about specific unused fns within the module. If specific fns are unused, either use them or remove them individually rather than blanket-suppressing the whole module.
4. Verify #[allow(dead_code)] on redact_git_volatile (line 21986) in test code is appropriate (it is #[cfg(test)] so dead_code in tests is acceptable)
5. Run: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- verify zero warnings

APPROACH: Remove each annotation one at a time, run clippy, see what fires. Fix the underlying issue (either use the code or remove it) rather than re-adding the suppression.

FILES: src/tui.rs
RISK: Low — worst case is clippy warnings that reveal genuinely unused code to remove.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.703721465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:23.507576871Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.7","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.703721465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.7","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:22.503455578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.7","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:23.011440891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-nqqq.7","depends_on_id":"bd-nqqq.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:23.506819548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-nqqq.8","title":"Final verification: full test suite, clippy, fmt after all stub resolutions","description":"BACKGROUND: This is the final gate task after all other beads in the epic are complete. It ensures nothing was broken across the cumulative changes.

DEPENDS ON: ALL other bd-nqqq.* tasks

WHAT TO DO:
1. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (must be clean)
2. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets (must be zero warnings)
3. cargo test --lib (run locally for snapshot tests — must be 1400+ tests, 0 failures)
4. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test conformance --test schema_validation --test e2e_robot_matrix --test cli_model_validation --test stress_fixtures --test admin_cli (all integration suites)
5. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_workspace_history (may have 2 known rch-only failures for watch tests — acceptable)
6. Verify: git grep -i 'allow(dead_code)' src/ — should show ONLY legitimate suppressions (test-only code, platform-specific no-ops)
7. Verify: git grep -i 'not implemented\|not supported' src/ — should show ZERO stubs
8. Verify: bv --robot-next (should still work correctly)
9. br sync --flush-only && git add . && git commit && git push

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Zero clippy warnings
- Zero fmt issues
- All tests pass (except known rch watch-mode flakes)
- No remaining stub/placeholder code in src/
- All #[allow(dead_code)] annotations are justified

But open_tutorial() at line 5546 is #[allow(dead_code)] because no production keybinding invokes it. Users can never see this tutorial.

WHAT TO DO:
1. src/tui.rs: Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from open_tutorial() (line 5546)
2. Add keybinding in the global key handler (around line 4558 area — the section that handles keys regardless of view mode):
   KeyCode::Char('t') if modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) => { self.open_tutorial(); }
   IMPORTANT: Verify Ctrl+T is not already bound by checking all KeyCode::Char('t') matches with CTRL modifier
3. Add ("Ctrl+T", "Tutorial") to the help text Global section (around line 7970)
4. Add one integration test: simulate Ctrl+T press from main view, assert modal_overlay == Some(ModalOverlay::Tutorial), then simulate any key press, assert modal_overlay == None

NOTES: The Tutorial content is static text defined at line 2593-2598. It covers modes (b/i/g/h), filters (o/c/r/a), navigation (j/k/Tab), and other keys (?/s/Enter/Esc/q). This is useful for first-time users but doesn't need to be auto-shown on first run (that can be a future enhancement).

+{"id":"bd-nqqq.5","title":"Wire PagesWizard modal to P keybinding in TUI","description":"BACKGROUND: The PagesWizard is a COMPLETE multi-step interactive wizard for configuring static pages export. It has full rendering (line 2623-2644), key handling (line 4240 + handle_pages_wizard_key at line 7779), step navigation, and option toggles. open_pages_wizard() at line 5560 and PagesWizardState::new() at line 2280 are both #[allow(dead_code)] because no keybinding invokes them. The wizard is only reachable from tests.

WHAT TO DO:
1. src/tui.rs: Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from open_pages_wizard() (line 5560) and PagesWizardState::new() (line 2281)
2. Add keybinding: KeyCode::Char('P') => { self.open_pages_wizard(); }
   Location: In the global key handler section (around line 4558).
   IMPORTANT: First verify P (uppercase) is not already bound by grepping for Char('P') in the key handler.
3. Add ("P", "Pages export wizard") to the help text Actions section (around line 7933)
4. Ensure the wizard is NOT accessible when a modal is already open (check that the global key handler's modal guard at the top prevents P from reaching the new binding)

COMPREHENSIVE TESTING:
- test_pages_wizard_opens_on_p_key: From main view, simulate Shift+P, assert modal_overlay == Some(ModalOverlay::PagesWizard(_))
- test_pages_wizard_p_ignored_during_search: Enter search mode (/), type P, verify it goes into search query not wizard
- test_pages_wizard_p_ignored_during_modal: Open help (?), press P, verify wizard does NOT open over help
- test_pages_wizard_esc_closes: Open wizard with P, press Esc, verify modal_overlay == None
- test_pages_wizard_tab_cycles_steps: Open wizard, press Tab repeatedly, verify step increments and wraps
- test_pages_wizard_help_text_includes_p: Verify the help text contains "Pages export wizard" in the Actions section
- Snapshot test: Capture wizard render at step 0 and verify it matches expected layout

NOTES: The wizard has 4 steps (step_count=4) controlled by PagesWizardState fields: step, export_dir, title, include_closed, include_history. Tab cycles steps, Enter confirms, Esc cancels. Existing tests at lines 21836-21927 cover step navigation thoroughly. The new tests focus on the KEYBINDING entry point and interaction guards.

+{"id":"bd-nqqq.6","title":"Wire Confirm modal into production use and remove dead_code annotations","description":"BACKGROUND: ModalOverlay::Confirm is FULLY IMPLEMENTED with rendering (line 2609-2621: title, message, Y/N with SemanticTone::Danger) and key handling (line 4226-4238: Y confirms, N/Esc cancels, stores in modal_confirm_result). But open_confirm() is #[allow(dead_code)] — nothing invokes it.

CRITICAL DESIGN NOTE: The existing quit-confirm flow (show_quit_confirm) uses Esc to CONFIRM quit. The Confirm modal uses Esc to CANCEL. These are incompatible — replacing show_quit_confirm with the Confirm modal would break the quit UX where users press Esc to quit. DO NOT replace the quit-confirm flow.

REVISED APPROACH: Wire the Confirm modal into PagesWizard completion as a real use case:
1. Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from open_confirm() (line 5551) and ModalOverlay::Confirm variant (line 2247)
2. When the PagesWizard reaches its final step and user presses Enter to export, open a Confirm dialog: open_confirm("Export Pages?", "This will write the pages bundle to disk. Continue?")
3. On Y: proceed with export. On N/Esc: return to wizard.
4. This gives the Confirm modal a real production use case without touching the quit flow.

ALTERNATIVE (simpler): If the PagesWizard integration is too complex, just remove the #[allow(dead_code)] annotations and add a code comment documenting that Confirm is a reusable primitive available for future destructive-action confirmations. The existing tests at lines 22529-22539 already exercise it.

TESTING:
- Unit test: open Confirm modal, simulate Y press, verify modal_confirm_result == Some(true)
- Unit test: open Confirm modal, simulate N press, verify modal_confirm_result == Some(false)
- Unit test: open Confirm modal, simulate Esc press, verify modal_confirm_result == Some(false)
- Integration test: verify quit flow STILL uses show_quit_confirm (regression guard)
- Snapshot test: Confirm modal renders with correct title, message, and Y/N prompt

+{"id":"bd-nqqq.7","title":"Clean up remaining #[allow(dead_code)] annotations on active code","description":"BACKGROUND: After wiring Tutorial (bd-nqqq.4), PagesWizard (bd-nqqq.5), and Confirm (bd-nqqq.6), several #[allow(dead_code)] annotations should be removable. This task is the systematic cleanup sweep.

DEPENDS ON: bd-nqqq.4, bd-nqqq.5, bd-nqqq.6

METHODOLOGY: Remove annotations ONE AT A TIME, run clippy after each removal. This reveals whether the annotation was suppressing a real warning or was unnecessary.

ITEM-BY-ITEM PLAN:

1. FlatFileEntry struct (line 2115):
   - Remove #[allow(dead_code)]. Run clippy.
   - Fields ARE read: name at line 13427, path at line 5596, is_dir at line 13427, change_count at line 13431, level at line 13431.
   - If clippy fires on specific fields, investigate whether the reads are behind #[cfg(test)].
   - Expected outcome: annotation was unnecessary.

2. ModalOverlay::Tutorial (line 2244):
   - Should already be removed by bd-nqqq.4. Verify. If still present, remove.

3. ModalOverlay::Confirm (line 2247):
   - Should already be removed by bd-nqqq.6. Verify. If still present, remove.

4. tokens module (line 696):
   - Remove #[allow(dead_code)] from the module-level annotation.
   - Run clippy to identify which specific internal functions/constants are unused.
   - For each unused item: determine if it SHOULD be used (missing integration) or is genuinely dead.
   - Remove genuinely dead items. Wire items that should be used.
   - IMPORTANT: Do NOT re-add the blanket suppression. Fix individual items.

5. redact_git_volatile (line 21986):
   - This is #[cfg(test)] code. Dead code in test modules is acceptable.
   - Verify it IS actually used in at least one test. If not, either add a test that uses it or remove it.

6. KeyTraceEntry (line 2459):
   - This is #[cfg(test)]. Check if all fields are read. If some are only written, that's a test logging struct pattern — acceptable.

TESTING:
- After ALL removals: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets (must be zero warnings)
- Run: cargo test --lib (must pass — no regressions from removing annotations)
- Verify with: grep -rn 'allow(dead_code)' src/ — list remaining, justify each

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Every remaining #[allow(dead_code)] in src/ must be one of:
- #[cfg(not(test))] conditional stub (e.g., debug_trace_len)
- #[cfg(not(unix))] platform no-op (e.g., configure_hook_command)
- #[cfg(test)] test-only code
No blanket module-level suppressions should remain.

+{"id":"bd-nqqq.8","title":"Final verification: full test suite, clippy, fmt after all stub resolutions","description":"BACKGROUND: Final gate task. Ensures ALL stub resolutions are correct and nothing regressed.

DEPENDS ON: ALL bd-nqqq.* tasks

VERIFICATION CHECKLIST (execute in order):

Phase 1 — Static Analysis:
1. cargo fmt --check (locally — fmt must NOT be run via rch)
2. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets (zero warnings)
3. git grep -rn 'allow(dead_code)' src/ | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]' | grep -v '#\[cfg(not(' — list remaining, justify each
4. git grep -in 'not implemented\|not supported\|stub\|placeholder\|todo\b' src/ — must be ZERO matches (confirms all stubs resolved)

Phase 2 — Unit Tests:
5. cargo test --lib (run LOCALLY for snapshot accuracy — expect 1400+ tests, 0 failures)
   Log: capture full output to /tmp/bvr_verification_lib.log
6. Check test count did not decrease: previous baseline was 1401 lib tests

Phase 3 — Integration Tests (all via rch):
7. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test conformance (79 tests)
8. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test schema_validation (61 tests)
9. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_robot_matrix (62 tests)
10. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation (60+ tests)
11. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test stress_fixtures (49 tests)
12. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test admin_cli (verify --check-update test passes with new behavior)
13. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_export_pages (20 tests — 2 watch-mode tests may fail on rch workers, that's known/acceptable)
14. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_workspace_history (34 tests)

Phase 4 — Functional Verification:
15. bv --robot-next (must not crash, produces valid JSON)
16. bv --robot-triage (must produce valid triage output)
17. bvr --check-update (must not print "not implemented" — should print version info)
18. bvr --version (must print version)
19. bvr --robot-help (must not mention --update or --rollback)

Phase 5 — Commit and Push:
20. br sync --flush-only
21. git add .beads/ src/ tests/ Cargo.toml Cargo.lock AGENTS.md
22. git commit with descriptive message
23. git push
24. Close bd-nqqq epic

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Zero clippy warnings
- Zero fmt issues
- All test suites pass (except 2 known rch watch-mode flakes)
- Test count >= 1401 (no test regressions)
- No remaining stub/placeholder strings in src/
- All #[allow(dead_code)] justified
- --check-update prints real version info, not "not implemented"
- --update and --rollback flags no longer exist
- Tutorial accessible via Ctrl+T
- PagesWizard accessible via P
- Confirm modal has #[allow(dead_code)] removed and is documented
